sections.label: Section 1658
sections.original: σαυτὴν ἄρʼ ἐγγὺς τῷδε συνθάψεις νεκρῷ .
sections.translation: Do so , and you will be making your own grave by his .

sections.label: Section 1659
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐκλεές τοι δύο φίλω κεῖσθαι πέλας .
sections.translation: A noble end , for two so near and dear to lie side by side !

sections.label: Section 1660
sections.original: λάζυσθε τήνδε κἀς δόμους κομίζετε .
sections.translation: Seize and take her inside .

sections.label: Section 1661
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ , ἐπεὶ τοῦδʼ οὐ μεθήσομαι νεκροῦ .
sections.translation: Oh , no ! For I will not let go of this corpse .

sections.label: Section 1662
sections.original: ἔκρινʼ ὁ δαίμων , παρθένʼ , οὐχ ἃ σοὶ δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: These are the god ’ s decrees , my girl , not what seems good to you .

sections.label: Section 1663
sections.original: κἀκεῖνο κέκριται , μὴ ἐφυβρίζεσθαι νεκρούς .
sections.translation: And this has been decreed , not to insult the dead .

sections.label: Section 1664
sections.original: ὡς οὔτις ἀμφὶ τῷδʼ ὑγρὰν θήσει κόνιν .
sections.translation: Be sure that no one will sprinkle over the corpse the moistened dust .

sections.label: Section 1665
sections.original: ναὶ πρός σε τῆσδε μητρὸς Ἰοκάστης , Κρέον .
sections.translation: O Creon , by my mother Jocasta , I implore you !

sections.label: Section 1666
sections.original: μάταια μοχθεῖς · οὐ γὰρ ἂν τύχοις τάδε .
sections.translation: Your labor is in vain ; you will not gain your prayer .

sections.label: Section 1667
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἀλλὰ νεκρῷ λουτρὰ περιβαλεῖν μʼ ἔα .
sections.translation: Let me only bathe the dead body .

sections.label: Section 1668
sections.original: ἓν τοῦτʼ ἂν εἴη τῶν ἀπορρήτων πόλει .
sections.translation: That would be part of what is forbidden by the city .

sections.label: Section 1669
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀμφὶ τραύματʼ ἄγρια τελαμῶνας βαλεῖν .
sections.translation: At least let me bandage the cruel wounds .

sections.label: Section 1670
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅπως σὺ τόνδε τιμήσεις νέκυν .
sections.translation: No ; you will never pay honor to this corpse .

sections.label: Section 1671
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἀλλὰ στόμα γε σὸν προσπτύξομαι .
sections.translation: O my dearest ! At least I will kiss your mouth .

sections.label: Section 1672
sections.original: οὐ μὴ ἐς γάμους σοὺς συμφορὰν κτήσῃ γόοις .
sections.translation: Do not let this mourning bring disaster on your marriage .

sections.label: Section 1673
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ γαμοῦμαι ζῶσα παιδὶ σῷ ποτε ;
sections.translation: Marriage ! Do you think I will marry your son while I am alive ?

sections.label: Section 1674
sections.original: πολλή σʼ ἀνάγκη · ποῖ γὰρ ἐκφεύξῃ λέχος ;
sections.translation: Indeed you must ; how will you escape the match ?

sections.label: Section 1675
sections.original: νὺξ ἆρʼ ἐκείνη Δαναΐδων μʼ ἕξει μίαν .
sections.translation: Then that night will find in me another Danaid bride !

sections.label: Section 1676
sections.original: εἶδες τὸ τόλμημʼ οἷον ἐξωνείδισεν ;
sections.translation: Do you see how boldly she reproaches me ?

sections.label: Section 1677
sections.original: ἴστω σίδηρος ὅρκιόν τέ μοι ξίφος .
sections.translation: Let the steel know , the sword be my witness !

sections.label: Section 1678
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐκπροθυμῇ τῶνδʼ ἀπηλλάχθαι γάμων ;
sections.translation: Why are you so eager to be released from this marriage ?

sections.label: Section 1679
sections.original: συμφεύξομαι τῷδʼ ἀθλιωτάτῳ πατρί .
sections.translation: I mean to share my hapless father ’ s exile .

sections.label: Section 1680
sections.original: γενναιότης σοι , μωρία δʼ ἔνεστί τις .
sections.translation: A noble spirit yours but there is some folly in it .

sections.label: Section 1681
sections.original: καὶ ξυνθανοῦμαί γʼ , ὡς μάθῃς περαιτέρω .
sections.translation: And I will share his death , I tell you further .

sections.label: Section 1682
sections.original: ἴθʼ , οὐ φονεύσεις παῖδʼ ἐμόν , λίπε χθόνα .
sections.translation: Go , leave the land ; you will not murder my son . Exit Creon .

sections.label: Section 1683
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , αἰνῶ μέν σε τῆς προθυμίας .
sections.translation: Daughter , for this loyal spirit I thank you .

sections.label: Section 1684
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ γαμοίμην , σὺ δὲ μόνος φεύγοις , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: How could I marry , while you went into exile alone , father ?

sections.label: Section 1685
sections.original: μένʼ εὐτυχοῦσα , τἄμʼ ἐγὼ στέρξω κακά .
sections.translation: Stay here and be happy ; I will bear my own load of sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1686
sections.original: καὶ τίς σε τυφλὸν ὄντα θεραπεύσει , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: And who will tend you in your blindness , father ?

sections.label: Section 1687
sections.original: πεσὼν ὅπου μοι μοῖρα κείσομαι πέδῳ .
sections.translation: Where fate appoints , there I will fall and lie down upon the ground .

sections.label: Section 1688
sections.original: ὁ δʼ Οἰδίπους ποῦ καὶ τὰ κλείνʼ αἰνίγματα ;
sections.translation: Where is Oedipus , and that famous riddle ?

sections.label: Section 1689
sections.original: ὄλωλʼ · ἓν ἦμάρ μʼ ὤλβισʼ , ἓν δʼ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: Lost ! One day blessed me , one destroyed me .

sections.label: Section 1690
sections.original: οὔκουν μετασχεῖν κἀμὲ δεῖ τῶν σῶν κακῶν ;
sections.translation: May I not also share your sorrows ?

sections.label: Section 1691
sections.original: αἰσχρὰ φυγὴ θυγατρὶ σὺν τυφλῷ πατρί .
sections.translation: To wander with her blinded father would be shameful for his daughter .

sections.label: Section 1692
sections.original: οὔ , σωφρονούσῃ γʼ , ἀλλὰ γενναία , πάτερ .
sections.translation: Not so , father , but glory , if she is discreet .

sections.label: Section 1693
sections.original: προσάγαγέ νύν με , μητρὸς ὡς ψαύσω σέθεν .
sections.translation: Lead me near , so that I may touch your mother ’ s corpse .

sections.label: Section 1694
sections.original: ἰδού , γεραιᾶς φιλτάτης ψαῦσον χερί .
sections.translation: There , embrace the aged form so dear to you .

sections.label: Section 1695
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ , ὦ ξυνάορʼ ἀθλιωτάτη .
sections.translation: O mother , o most wretched wife !

sections.label: Section 1696
sections.original: οἰκτρὰ πρόκειται , πάντʼ ἔχουσʼ ὁμοῦ κακά .
sections.translation: Pitiably she lies , who suffered every evil at once .

sections.label: Section 1697
sections.original: Ἐτεοκλέους δὲ πτῶμα Πολυνείκους τε ποῦ ;
sections.translation: Where are the corpses of Eteocles , and of Polyneices ?

sections.label: Section 1698
sections.original: τώδʼ ἐκτάδην σοι κεῖσθον ἀλλήλοιν πέλας .
sections.translation: Here they both lie , stretched out side by side .

sections.label: Section 1699
sections.original: πρόσθες τυφλὴν χεῖρʼ ἐπὶ πρόσωπα δυστυχῆ .
sections.translation: Lay my blind hand upon their poor faces .

sections.label: Section 1700
sections.original: ἰδού , θανόντων σῶν τέκνων ἅπτου χερί .
sections.translation: There , touch the dead , your children .

sections.label: Section 1701
sections.original: ὦ φίλα πεσήματʼ ἄθλιʼ ἀθλίου πατρός .
sections.translation: O dear fallen sons , sad offspring of a sad father !

sections.label: Section 1702
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατον δῆτʼ ὄνομα Πολυνείκους ἐμοί .
sections.translation: O my brother Polyneices , name most dear to me !

sections.label: Section 1703
sections.original: νῦν χρησμός , ὦ παῖ , Λοξίου περαίνεται .
sections.translation: Now the oracle of Loxias is being fulfilled , my child .

sections.label: Section 1704
sections.original: ὁ ποῖος ; ἀλλʼ ἦ πρὸς κακοῖς ἐρεῖς κακά ;
sections.translation: What oracle ? Do you have further woes to tell ?

sections.label: Section 1705
sections.original: ἐν ταῖς Ἀθήναις κατθανεῖν μʼ ἀλώμενον .
sections.translation: That I should die in Athens after a life of wandering .

sections.label: Section 1706
sections.original: ποῦ ; τίς σε πύργος Ἀτθίδος προσδέξεται ;
sections.translation: Where ? What fenced town in Attica will take you in ?

sections.label: Section 1707
sections.original: ἱερὸς Κολωνός , δώμαθʼ ἱππίου θεοῦ .
sections.translation: Hallowed Colonus , home of the god of horses . Come then , attend on your blind father , since you are eager to share his exile .

sections.label: Section 1710
sections.original: ἴθʼ ἐς φυγὰν τάλαιναν · ὄρεγε χέρα φίλαν ,
sections.translation: Go to unhappy exile ; stretch forth your dear hand , my old father , taking me to guide you , like a breeze that guides the ships .

sections.label: Section 1713
sections.original: ἰδοὺ ἰδού , πορεύομαι ·
sections.translation: See , I am advancing ; be my guide , my poor child .

sections.label: Section 1716
sections.original: γενόμεθα γενόμεθʼ , ἄθλιαί
sections.translation: I am , I am ! The saddest maiden of all in Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1718
sections.original: πόθι γεραιὸν ἴχνος τίθημι ;
sections.translation: Where am I placing my aged step ? Bring my staff , child .

sections.label: Section 1720
sections.original: τᾷδε τᾷδε βᾶθί μοι ,
sections.translation: This way , this way , come to me , place your steps here , like a dream in your strength .

sections.label: Section 1723
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ , δυστυχεστάτας φυγὰς
sections.translation: Oh , oh , driving the old man in most wretched flight from the country !

sections.label: Section 1725
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ , δεινὰ δείνʼ ἐγὼ τλάς .
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! the terrible sorrows I have endured !

sections.label: Section 1726
sections.original: τί τλάς ; τί τλάς ; οὐχ ὁρᾷ Δίκα κακούς ,
sections.translation: Why do you speak of enduring ? Justice does not see the wicked , and does not requite follies .

sections.label: Section 1728
sections.original: ὅδʼ εἰμὶ μοῦσαν ὃς ἐπὶ καλ-
sections.translation: I am the one who came into high songs of victory ,

sections.label: Section 1730
sections.original: μειξο παρθένου κόρας
sections.translation: because I guessed the baffling riddle of the girl , half-maiden .
